exemplar.title: Prompt: 26. Does our knowledge depend on our interactions with other knowers?

detailedExemplarData.title: Prompt: 26. Does our knowledge depend on our interactions with other knowers?

strengths: Clear identification of three specific, realâworld objects with immediate contexts (pointe shoes, electric guitar, teacher photograph).
The chosen IA prompt is prominently displayed and each object is directly linked to the prompt about interaction with other knowers.
Personal narratives provide basic justification for inclusion, grounding the exhibition in genuine experiences of mentorship and peer/family interaction.
weaknesses: Commentary remains largely descriptive and anecdotal, lacking deeper analytical engagement with the knowledge question.
Repetitive references to discipline, compassion, and patience across objects limit precision and clarity.
Little or no use of TOK terminology (e.g., ways of knowing, shared knowledge) and absence of a central knowledge question to frame the analysis.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission meets the descriptors for the 3â4 band: it identifies three concrete objects and connects each to the IA prompt, showing a basic awareness of how interaction shapes knowledge. However, the commentary is surface-level and relies almost exclusively on personal narrative without broader evidence or critical TOK concepts. There is repetition across objects and minimal analytical depthâconnections to the prompt are asserted rather than explored. Given these strengths and limitations, a score of 4 accurately reflects the workâs position at the upper end of the 3â4 band, acknowledging its clear object selection and contextual links while noting its superficial analysis and lack of explicit TOK framework.

annotations.comment: The prompt is prominently displayed, ensuring the exhibition is directly anchored to the IA question.
There is no explicit articulation of a central knowledge question to frame the exploration of interactions and knowers.
Consider adding a brief introduction that outlines how each chosen object will illustrate the role of interaction in knowledge formation.
Engage more directly with TOK terminology (e.g., âways of knowing,â âshared knowledgeâ) early to signal critical depth.
How will you define key terms such as âknower,â âinteraction,â and âknowledgeâ to avoid ambiguity in your analysis?
Outline the structure of your commentary at the outset to guide the reader through each objectâs contribution.
The selection of pointe shoes is effective: it is a concrete, personal object that grounds the prompt.
Clear symbolism is presented: the pointe shoes effectively represent dedication and achievement.
The commentary remains largely descriptive; it needs deeper analysis of how interaction with knowers actively shapes your knowledge.
Integrate TOK concepts (e.g., âshared knowledge,â âpersonal knowledge,â âways of knowingâ) when discussing discipline and problem-solving.
Repetition of âdiscipline, problem-solving, social skillsâ echoes across objects without linking back to the prompt analytically.
How might you include an external example of mentorship or peer interaction beyond your personal narrative for balance?
Consider framing âthe journey to pointe shoesâ as evidence of how interactive guidance contributes to evolving knowledge.
The guitar image conveys personal significance and a concrete link to the prompt via familial interaction.
Reliance on anecdotal evidence limits the depth of your analysis of how the brother functions as a knower.
Expand beyond your brotherâs perspective: include how his guitar teacher or band members contribute to your understanding of music.
Analyze the knowledge implications of autism in music-making: how does your brotherâs condition shape the knowledge he offers?
The discussion is still largely descriptive; consider critically reflecting on how each interaction changes your own knowledge framework.
Could you reference a published study on music education to support or contrast your personal account?
Your teacher photograph is well chosen and highlights the role of mentorship in knowledge transmission.
The narrative remains descriptive and would benefit from a critical examination of how teacherâstudent interactions construct knowledge.
You might consider discussing potential biases or limitations in the teacherâs perspective as a knower.
Incorporate TOK distinctions such as shared versus personal knowledge when evaluating the teacherâs role.
The concluding description of skills remains generic; refine this to show precisely how these skills resulted from interaction.
How will you tie together the three objects in a cohesive concluding synthesis that compares their contributions?
Consider adding a final section that explicitly compares and contrasts how each object demonstrates the role of interaction in knowledge formation.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
